      this is, or whether calling it an ocean is a kindness it does not deserve.\n\nI

    title: 'Captain''s Log II: Survivor'
    author: Unknown
    pages:
    - '"The rain is the worst of it.\n\nIt falls like any rain should fall — grey,
      patient, from clouds that look like any clouds I remember — but it hisses
      when it lands and my wheat curls black where the drops strike. I sheltered
      under acacia leaves for an hour today and counted the holes it left.\n\nRain
      is supposed to be water. This is not water. I do not know what word to use
      instead.\n\nI have started calling the sky a liar. It feels better than being
      afraid of it."'
    generation: 3

    title: 'Captain''s Log III: Mariner'
    author: Unknown
    pages:
    - '"I dove today. Long potion, a prayer, a plunge.\n\nDown there, in the prismarine
      canyons, I found something that should not be there: a length of rusted metal
      pipe, straight as a ruler, bolted at the joints. Not coral. Not stone. Made
      by hands.\n\nSo someone built here. Or will build here. Or did, a very long
      time ago, before whatever poisoned the sea finished its work.\n\nI am not
      the first castaway on this island. I am only the latest."'

    title: 'Captain''s Log IV: Navigator'
    author: Unknown
    pages:
    - '"The portal burns purple and the nether burns red and neither of them burns
      the way the sea burns. There is a lesson in that, if I had the stomach to
      read it.\n\nI have been thinking about the word ACID.\n\nAcid is a thing
      that dissolves other things. Acid is what happens when something has gone
      wrong at the level of the world itself. Acid is the ash-taste of a chemistry
      I am not supposed to understand.\n\nWas this a garden once? Was the sea
      blue? Did somebody, somewhere, do this to it — or did the sea do it to
      itself?\n\nThe nether does not have these questions. Maybe that is why I
      keep going back."'
    generation: 3

    title: 'Captain''s Log V: Captain'
    author: Unknown
    pages:
    - '"I built a conduit today.\n\nIt hums at a frequency I know I should not
      recognise, and yet I do — deep in my teeth, the way an old machine hums
      when you stand too close to it in a quiet room. I have never stood in a
      quiet room with an old machine. I would swear to that. And yet.\n\nThere
      is a memory in me that is not mine.\n\nI am beginning to think that whatever
      broke this world broke it long enough ago that the word HISTORY does not
      apply. There is no before. There is the sea, and the rain the sea exhales,
      and the silt at the bottom of it all where something very old is still
      dreaming."'
    generation: 3

    title: 'Captain''s Log VI: Leviathan'
    author: Unknown
    pages:
    - '"The elder guardians are not guarding the ruins.\n\nThey are guarding
      something under the ruins. I understand that now. I have been down far
      enough to feel the shape of it — the way a diver feels a current before
      the current takes them. A slow turning. A breath so vast it takes seasons
      to complete.\n\nI think the acid is not a wound. I think the acid is a
      seal. I think it has been holding the thing below the silt inside its own
      grave, one dissolving century at a time, and nobody built this sea — the
      sea built itself, around a prisoner, to be a prison.\n\nI do not know if
      I should be trying to break it or thank it."'
    generation: 3

    title: 'Captain''s Log VII: Admiral'
    author: Unknown
    pages:
    - '"I will never know what this place is.\n\nI have stopped asking. The
      questions were for the man who woke up on a strip of sand afraid of the
      rain, and I am not that man any more. I have eaten from this poisoned
      garden so long that I am part of its chemistry.\n\nMaybe this is Earth
      after everyone is gone. Maybe this is Earth before anyone has arrived.
      Maybe it is a mirror of a world I was never born into. The sea does not
      care which. The sea only hisses, and rises, and waits.\n\nI leave these
      logs for the next castaway. If there is one. If there has ever been anyone
      but me.\n\nThe tide is turning. I think I will go out to meet it."'
    generation: 3
